originalTitle: Trump walks out of interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after clash over election claims
summary: Kristen Welker questioned Trump’s allegations that races for California governor and 2020 president were ‘rigged’
content: Donald Trump walked out of an interview with NBC’s Meet the Press after he repeatedly made false claims that the US 2020 presidential elections was rigged in 2020 and questions around compensation for those charged in the January 6 insurrection. The US president’s abrupt exit came during a tense exchange between himself and NBC’s Kristen Welker during a Friday interview in Wisconsin that aired on Sunday. Trump falsely claimed that the California gubernatorial race was “rigged” while asserting untrue claims of cheating in the 2020 US presidential elections. “It’s four days and they aren’t even close to counting [ballots],” Trump argued, as Welker argued that is standard for California’s election process. Trump also claimed that Republican candidates in the state weren’t performing well. Current polls have forecasted a tight race between Democratic frontrunner Xavier Becerra, who advanced to the general November election, and Republican politician Steve Hilton. When Welker asked the president for any evidence on the gubernatorial race being fraudulent, he also accused the veteran reporter of being “crooked”. “They’re crooked, just like you’re crooked, your press is crooked. And Meet the Press is crooked,” said Trump. Welker then defended herself and tried to ask additional questions, with Trump replying: “You’re either crooked or you’re stupid. You play right into their hands with this crap. You know that these elections are rigged. Your network knows that they’re rigged.” Trump then brought up previously repeated claims that he won the 2020 US presidential election, assertions that remain unverified. When Welker later tried to ask additional questions, Trump continued to assert that NBC was “crooked” and ended the interview. “Let’s call it quits because I’ve had enough,” said Trump, taking off his microphone. “Thank you, darling. Have a good time.” As Welker reminded Trump that she traveled to Wisconsin for the interview, Trump replied: “I’ve sat in the rain with you for an hour. On and off in the rain and I’ve given you enough time. You oughta straighten out your press.” Earlier in the interview, Trump also became irritated as Welker asked if those who pleaded guilty to assaulting police officers during the January 6 riots would be eligible for funding included in Trump’s controversial “anti-weaponization” fund. Trump claimed – sans evidence – that rioters were actually invited into the US Capitol by FBI agents and took plea deals due to fears of longer prison sentences. “You know why they pleaded guilty? Because they were told they were going to jail for 15 years … because they were frightened. They were down. They were ushered into a building,” Trump said, refusing to answer if such individuals should receive taxpayer funding. A representative of NBC News did not return the Guardian’s request for comment on Trump’s exit.
chineseTitle: 因选举主张发生冲突后，特朗普退出接受 NBC 会见媒体采访
chineseSummary: 克里斯汀·韦尔克 (Kristen Welker) 质疑特朗普关于加州州长和 2020 年总统竞选被“操纵”的指控
chineseContent: 唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 多次虚假声称 2020 年美国 2020 年总统选举被操纵，并质疑 1 月 6 日叛乱中被指控的人的赔偿问题，随后退出了 NBC 节目《与媒体会面》的采访。美国总统突然退出，是在周日播出的威斯康星州周五采访中，他与美国全国广播公司（NBC）的克里斯汀·韦尔克（Kristen Welker）进行了紧张的交流。特朗普谎称加州州长竞选被“操纵”，同时还声称2020年美国总统选举作弊。特朗普辩称，“已经四天了，他们甚至还没有接近计票”，而韦尔克则认为这是加州选举程序的标准。特朗普还声称该州的共和党候选人表现不佳。目前的民意调查预测，在 11 月大选中晋级的民主党领跑者泽维尔·贝塞拉 (Xavier Becerra) 与共和党政治家史蒂夫·希尔顿 (Steve Hilton) 之间的竞争将十分激烈。当韦尔克向总统索要州长竞选舞弊的任何证据时，他还指责这位资深记者“不诚实”。 “他们是歪曲的，就像你是歪曲的一样，你的媒体也是歪曲的。会见媒体也是歪曲的，”特朗普说。韦尔克随后为自己辩护，并试图提出更多问题，特朗普回答道：“你要么不诚实，要么很愚蠢。你用这些废话正中他们的下怀。你知道这些选举被操纵了。你的网络知道他们被操纵了。”特朗普随后提出了此前多次声称他赢得 2020 年美国总统大选的说法，但这些说法尚未得到证实。当韦尔克后来试图提出更多问题时，特朗普继续声称 NBC 是“不诚实的”并结束了采访。 “我们就此罢手吧，因为我已经受够了。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
